creator: hrs@FreeBSD.org
text:
----Security_Multipart(Tue_Dec__6_03_18_30_2011_519)--
Content-Type: Text/Plain; charset=us-ascii
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

"Mikhail T." <mi+thun@aldan.algebra.com> wrote
  in <4EDCF6AC.7000104@aldan.algebra.com>:

mi> On 05.12.2011 11:38, Hiroki Sato wrote:
mi> >   Can you show me your installed libdata/pkgconfig/librtmp.pc?
mi> >   @CRYPTO_REQ@ should be replaced with "", so removing Requires: line
mi> >   will make no difference.  I want to know if you have something like
mi> >   "Requires: foo" in librtmp.pc.
mi>
mi>    prefix=/opt
mi>    exec_prefix=${prefix}
mi>    libdir=${exec_prefix}/lib
mi>    incdir=${prefix}/include
mi>
mi>    Name: librtmp
mi>    Description: RTMP implementation
mi>    Version: v2.3
mi>    Requires: libssl,libcrypto
mi>    URL: http://rtmpdump.mplayerhq.hu
mi>    Libs: -L${libdir} -lrtmp -lz
mi>    Cflags: -I${incdir}

 Hmm, this is odd because this means the patch
 files/patch-librtmp-librtmp.pc.in was not applied...  Is it correct
 that your rtmpdump's version is rtmpdump-2.3_1 and the librtmp.pc has
 the "Requires: libssl,libcrypto" line even after reinstallation of
 the port?  And if correct, could you send me a log of "make clean &&
 make && make install" in multimedia/rtmpdump?  Thank you.

-- Hiroki
